benchmark_details.name: FELM (Factual Evaluation of large Language Models)
benchmark_details.overview: FELM is a benchmark for evaluating the factuality of text generated by large language models, incorporating responses from multiple domains with fine-grained annotations for factual accuracy and error types.

purpose_and_intended_users.goal: To provide a standardized evaluation framework for assessing the factuality of large language model outputs across diverse domains.
purpose_and_intended_users.limitations: While we have invested significant effort in this work, there are still some limitations to our study: we did not explore additional application scenarios, such as code generation.

data.source: Responses generated by large language models (LLMs) such as ChatGPT, annotated by expert annotators.
data.annotation: Annotated by expert human annotators with labels for factuality, error type, and reference links.

methodology.calculation: Metrics are calculated based on the evaluation of F1 Score, Precision, Recall, and Balanced Accuracy across different evaluators.
methodology.interpretation: Performance is measured on the ability to accurately assess factual errors in generated text across multiple established metrics.
methodology.baseline_results: The benchmark reveals that most detectors performed unsatisfactorily, with only some models achieving F1 scores greater than 40.
methodology.validation: Results must be validated through human annotation and consistency checks.
